title: Prolanis Follow-up — Medication Review
severitySummary: ADVISORY — 2 monitored interactions, no blocking alerts

streamBlocks.text: Clinical Decision Support — Medication Interaction Analysis
58-year-old male on 6 medications for Prolanis (T2DM + HTN). eGFR 78 mL/min — renal function adequate. New complaint: orthostatic dizziness × 1 week.

streamBlocks.title: Captopril + Aspirin
streamBlocks.text: Theoretical reduced ACE-I efficacy; renal vasoconstriction risk in dehydration.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Aspirin may attenuate ACE-I mediated prostaglandin synthesis; clinically modest at low-dose aspirin.
streamBlocks.recommendation: Monitor BP and renal function. Ensure adequate hydration (1.5 L/day as advised). No change required today.
streamBlocks.reference: Advisory level — not blocking. Common co-prescription in cardiovascular risk patients.

streamBlocks.title: Simvastatin 20 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg
streamBlocks.text: Current Simvastatin dose at FDA advisory ceiling when combined with Amlodipine.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4, increasing Simvastatin exposure and myopathy risk at doses >20 mg.
streamBlocks.recommendation: Maintain Simvastatin at ≤20 mg (current dose acceptable). Monitor for myalgia. Do not increase Simvastatin dose.
streamBlocks.reference: FDA 2011 Drug Safety Communication — Simvastatin dose limit with Amlodipine.

streamBlocks.title: Glimepiride in 58 y/o — monitor if eGFR declines
streamBlocks.text: Sulfonylurea acceptable at current eGFR 78; re-evaluate if eGFR falls below 60.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Reduced renal clearance of active metabolites increases hypoglycaemia risk as eGFR declines.
streamBlocks.recommendation: Continue current dose. Recheck eGFR at next Prolanis visit. Consider DPP-4 inhibitor if eGFR <60.
streamBlocks.reference: Beers Criteria — caution with sulfonylureas when eGFR <60 or age >65.

streamBlocks.title: Polypharmacy contribution to orthostatic symptoms
streamBlocks.text: 5 drugs affecting BP/glycaemia — likely contributor to reported morning dizziness.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Combined antihypertensive effect (Amlodipine + Captopril) with orthostatic challenge.
streamBlocks.recommendation: Non-pharmacological measures advised (slow rise, 30s count). Planned Captopril → Lisinopril switch if BP uncontrolled next visit.
streamBlocks.reference: Doctor stated conditional switch to Lisinopril 10 mg OD for adherence if BP remains elevated.

references.title: FDA Drug Safety Communication (2011)
references.detail: Simvastatin 80 mg contraindicated with Amlodipine; limit to 20 mg.

references.title: ACE-I + Antiplatelet Interaction Review
references.detail: Low-dose aspirin co-prescription with ACE-I — clinical significance and monitoring.
